title: Current Studio Architecture
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Defines the verified current Fully Modular StudioDB architecture and its operating layers.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document defines the verified current architecture of the Two Marshalls Studios Operating System (TMS-OS). It records how the studio's portal, structured data, documentation, search, source code, and permanent human-readable records relate to one another at the current historical recovery checkpoint.
This document describes the architecture that is verified now. Historical changes belong in the separate Architecture Evolution record.

sections.title: Architecture Control Rule
paragraphs: The current architecture must be described from verified system behavior and approved records, not assumptions about future work.

TMS-OS and StudioDB govern structured studio records. Google Workspace provides the permanent human-readable documentation layer. GitHub preserves source code and project history. Chat, screenshots, temporary files, and local working copies may provide evidence or working context, but they must not become competing permanent sources of truth.

sections.title: Current Architecture Summary
paragraphs: The verified current architecture is:
Fully Modular StudioDB

The architecture is organized around a shared Studio Operating System in which structured studio information is separated into modular data files and combined into a common studio data object for use across the Studio Portal.

sections.title: Core Architecture Layers
items: Studio Portal — visible command center and user-facing operating interface.
Modular studio-data files — separated data sources organized by studio information area.
Shared render layer — reusable rendering behavior for portal content.
Shared search engine — cross-department search across supported studio knowledge.
GitHub — source code and project history preservation.
Google Headquarters — permanent human-readable documentation and operational reference layer.
Enhancement Ideas Log — controlled holding location for future improvements that are not active work.

sections.title: Studio Portal Layer
paragraphs: The Studio Portal is the visible command center for Two Marshalls Studios. It presents studio status, current work, system health, notifications, commands, projects, progress, departments, documentation, and shared search.
The portal is not intended to create separate copies of studio truth. Its role is to render and expose information from the governed studio data structure.

sections.title: StudioDB Layer
paragraphs: StudioDB is the structured data foundation of TMS-OS.
The verified stable checkpoint contains:
The last completed development milestone, MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB, established the current modular architecture.

sections.title: Modular Data Structure
paragraphs: The current architecture separates studio data into modular files rather than maintaining one large permanent data file.
The modular data sources are combined into the shared studio data object used by the portal. This allows the system to remain organized while still presenting a unified operating view.

sections.title: Shared Rendering Architecture
paragraphs: The portal uses shared rendering behavior so supported content can be displayed through common components and layouts rather than requiring unrelated page-specific systems.
This architecture supports consistency across studio areas and reduces the risk that individual pages become separate sources of truth.

sections.title: Shared Search Architecture
paragraphs: The shared search engine is a verified cross-department capability.
The search architecture is intended to search supported studio knowledge across departments rather than remaining isolated to a single page.

sections.title: Documentation Architecture
paragraphs: The documentation architecture has two distinct governed layers:
These layers must support one another without becoming competing sources of truth.
The permanent procedures governing this relationship include PROC-003 - Documentation Update Procedure, DOC-STATE-001 - Documentation State, and DOC-INDEX-001 - Documentation Index.
items: Structured records governed by TMS-OS and StudioDB.
Permanent human-readable documents published to the approved Google Headquarters structure.

sections.title: Google Headquarters Architecture
paragraphs: The verified permanent top-level Google Headquarters structure is:
This structure is complete. Population of the permanent human-readable documentation layer remains in progress.

sections.title: Source Code and Project History
paragraphs: GitHub preserves source code and project history. The studio architecture must remain accessible from multiple workstations and must not depend on one computer for important work.
Local files may be used during active work, but important source code, records, and permanent documentation must be preserved in their approved shared systems.

sections.title: Current Architecture Relationships
items: TMS-OS provides the overall studio operating framework.
Modular data files separate major data responsibilities.
The shared data object combines supported modular data for portal use.
The render layer displays governed information.
The shared search engine finds supported knowledge across studio areas.
GitHub preserves code and project history.
Google Headquarters preserves permanent human-readable documentation.
ENH-LOG-001 preserves deferred improvements without interrupting approved work.

sections.title: Current Architecture Boundaries
paragraphs: The following must not become independent permanent sources of truth:
These items may provide evidence, recovery material, or temporary working context, but verified knowledge must be reconciled into the governed architecture.
items: Files available only on one workstation

sections.title: Current Architecture Status
items: Last completed development milestone: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB
Current active milestone: MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery

sections.title: Change Control
paragraphs: Architecture changes must not be introduced during unrelated work or bug-fix activity unless Stephen explicitly approves a separate path.

A proposed architecture improvement must be captured as an enhancement when it is not part of approved current work. Approved architecture changes must later be preserved in the appropriate decision, architecture evolution, work session, milestone, and documentation records.

sections.title: Relationship to Other Permanent Records
items: STATE-001 records the current system state.
ROADMAP-001 records completed, active, and controlled future direction.
HIST-001 records the verified historical sequence.
DOC-INDEX-001 controls the permanent document index.
The future Architecture Evolution record will preserve verified changes from earlier architecture states to the current architecture.

sections.title: Current Architecture Completion Standard
paragraphs: The current architecture record is complete when it accurately describes the verified operating layers, their responsibilities, their relationships, and their boundaries without inventing unsupported components or future systems.

sections.title: Permanent Statement
paragraphs: The current Two Marshalls Studios architecture is a Fully Modular StudioDB operating model. Structured records, portal rendering, shared search, source control, and permanent human-readable documentation must remain coordinated so the studio can work across locations without losing history or creating competing sources of truth.
